questions.question: Which SQL command is used to modify existing data in a table?
questions.answer: UPDATE

questions.question: Which SQL command removes rows from a table?
questions.answer: DELETE

questions.question: What is the golden rule before running an UPDATE or DELETE statement?
options: Always back up the entire database first
Always include a WHERE clause unless you intentionally want to affect every row
Always run the command twice to confirm it works
questions.answer: Always include a WHERE clause unless you intentionally want to affect every row

questions.question: What does the following statement do? DELETE FROM students;
options: Deletes only the first row from students
Deletes every row from the students table
questions.answer: Deletes every row from the students table

questions.question: What is the recommended safety step before running a DELETE statement?
options: Run a SELECT with the same WHERE clause to preview affected rows
Create an index on the table
questions.answer: Run a SELECT with the same WHERE clause to preview affected rows

questions.question: Which clause in an UPDATE statement specifies the new values for columns?
questions.answer: SET

questions.question: What does this query do? UPDATE students SET gpa = 3.8 WHERE id = 1;
options: Inserts a new student with GPA 3.8
Changes the GPA to 3.8 for the student with id 1
questions.answer: Changes the GPA to 3.8 for the student with id 1

questions.question: How do you update multiple columns in a single UPDATE statement?
options: Separate column assignments with commas in the SET clause
Use the AND keyword between assignments
questions.answer: Separate column assignments with commas in the SET clause

questions.question: What does the CASE expression allow you to do in an UPDATE statement?
options: Create a new table based on conditions
Set different values based on different conditions in one statement
Delete rows that match a condition
questions.answer: Set different values based on different conditions in one statement

questions.question: What keyword ends a CASE expression?
questions.answer: END

questions.question: In a CASE expression, which keyword introduces each condition?
questions.answer: WHEN

questions.question: In a CASE expression, which keyword specifies the value to assign when a condition is true?
questions.answer: THEN

questions.question: In a CASE expression, what does the ELSE clause handle?
options: Rows that match the first WHEN condition
Rows that do not match any of the WHEN conditions
Rows that have NULL values only
questions.answer: Rows that do not match any of the WHEN conditions

questions.question: What does this query do? DELETE FROM enrollments WHERE student_id IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE major = 'Art');
options: Deletes all students who major in Art
Deletes enrollments for students whose major is Art
Updates the major of Art students
questions.answer: Deletes enrollments for students whose major is Art

questions.question: What is the purpose of a subquery in a DELETE statement's WHERE clause?
options: To create a new table for deleted rows
To identify which rows to delete based on data from another table
To count how many rows will be deleted
To automatically back up deleted rows
questions.answer: To identify which rows to delete based on data from another table

questions.question: What does BEGIN TRANSACTION do?
options: Starts a group of statements that will succeed or fail together
Backs up the database to a file
questions.answer: Starts a group of statements that will succeed or fail together

questions.question: What does the COMMIT command do in a transaction?
options: Undoes all changes made in the transaction
Saves all changes made in the transaction permanently
questions.answer: Saves all changes made in the transaction permanently

questions.question: What does the ROLLBACK command do in a transaction?
options: Exports the transaction to a file
Undoes all changes made in the transaction
questions.answer: Undoes all changes made in the transaction

questions.question: Why are transactions critical when performing multiple related changes?
options: They ensure all changes succeed together or none take effect
They allow multiple users to edit simultaneously
questions.answer: They ensure all changes succeed together or none take effect

questions.question: What would happen if you run UPDATE students SET gpa = 4.0; without a WHERE clause?
options: Only the first student's GPA would change
Every student's GPA would be set to 4.0
questions.answer: Every student's GPA would be set to 4.0

questions.question: Which SQL operator can be used in a subquery to check that a student has no enrollments?
questions.answer: NOT EXISTS

questions.question: What is the correct syntax to delete rows where grade is 'F'?
questions.answer: DELETE FROM enrollments WHERE grade = 'F';

questions.question: What happens to enrollments if you delete a student and the enrollments table has a foreign key referencing student_id with no ON DELETE action?
options: The enrollments are automatically updated to NULL
The delete may be blocked or orphan rows may remain depending on the database configuration
The enrollments table is dropped entirely
questions.answer: The delete may be blocked or orphan rows may remain depending on the database configuration

questions.question: Which of the following correctly updates grades using a subquery?
options: UPDATE enrollments SET grade = 'A' WHERE student_id IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.9);
UPDATE enrollments SET grade = 'A' IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.9);
UPDATE enrollments WHERE student_id IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.9) SET grade = 'A';
UPDATE enrollments SET grade = 'A' FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.9;
questions.answer: UPDATE enrollments SET grade = 'A' WHERE student_id IN (SELECT id FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.9);

questions.question: In a bank transfer scenario, why would you use a transaction for debiting one account and crediting another?
options: To ensure both the debit and credit happen together or neither happens, preventing lost money
To allow multiple transfers at the same time
questions.answer: To ensure both the debit and credit happen together or neither happens, preventing lost money
